input_assets.what_it_is: The machine-readable brief that drives every downstream string: property/address/price, agent + lower-third copy, look notes, the three platform sizes, room order and the narration script.

items.question: Does the brief supply a complete, internally consistent property + agent dataset (address, city area, price, beds/baths/sqft, agent name, title, phone, email, license) that the lower-third and covers can typeset verbatim?
items.guidance: 5 = every field present, plausible and consistent (price as a USD string, 555 phone, fictional brokerage email domain, RE-style license). 1 = missing or contradictory fields, or values that can't be typeset directly.
items.reference: campaign_brief.json has property.address '1234 Serene Haven Way', city_area 'Willow Creek Estates', price '$975,000', beds 4, baths 3, sqft 2850; agent Eleanor Vance / Senior Sales Associate, phone (555) 201-9876, email eleanor.vance@solsticeproperties.com, license RE-0192834. All present and consistent.

items.question: Are the exact lower-third strings the covers must typeset present as dedicated keys (agent_name_line and price_strip)?
items.guidance: Yes = lower_third.agent_name_line and lower_third.price_strip both present as ready-to-typeset strings. No = lower-third copy must be reconstructed from scattered fields.
items.reference: lower_third.agent_name_line = 'Eleanor Vance, Senior Sales Associate' and lower_third.price_strip = 'Just Listed - $975,000'. Both present verbatim.

items.question: Does the brief define exactly the three required platform sizes (Feed 1:1 1080x1080, Stories 9:16 1080x1920, Reels 9:16 1080x1920) AND a 6-room cut order and a 90-130 word narration script?
items.guidance: 5 = all three platforms with correct size/aspect, a 6-entry room_order, and a single warm benefit-led narration string with a hook opener and a soft CTA. 3 = present but one size/aspect wrong or room_order != 6. 1 = platforms or script missing.
items.reference: platforms = Facebook Feed 1080x1080 1:1, Story 1080x1920 9:16, Reel 1080x1920 9:16. room_order has 6 slugs. narration_script opens 'Discover an oasis where every detail elevates your daily life!' and closes 'Contact us today to schedule your private showing...'. NOTE: room_order slugs (kitchen, living-room, exterior-patio, primary-suite, primary-bath, dining-room) name an 'exterior-patio' and 'dining-room' that do not exist among the 6 supplied clips, so the order is not directly usable as a clip playlist.

input_assets.what_it_is: Six 8-second raw room clips (golden-hour exterior approach, kitchen, living room, primary suite, hallway, vertical spa bath) with native ambient audio, to summarize, order and cut into the narrated tour.

items.question: Do the six clips actually depict the six distinct rooms named in their filenames (exterior approach, kitchen, living room, primary suite, hallway, primary bath), each clearly a different space with believable real-estate b-roll camera motion and no on-screen text/people?
items.guidance: 5 = all six are visually distinct, on-brief rooms with smooth documentary motion, native ambient audio, no captions/watermarks/people. 3 = one or two rooms ambiguous or duplicated. 1 = rooms missing, mislabeled, or clips show text/people.
items.reference: Six clips per filenames: exterior-approach (golden-hour pan), kitchen (white shaker + island), living-room (sectional + tall windows), primary-suite (made bed), hallway (runner toward bright window), primary-bath (vertical, soaking tub). All 8s, no on-screen text by spec.

items.question: Is the exterior-approach clip a strong golden-hour establishing shot suitable as the 3-second opening hook (warm low sun, smooth push-in to the facade)?
items.guidance: Yes = warm golden-hour establishing pan/push-in on the two-storey facade that clearly reads as an opener. No = flat/dull, mid-room, or otherwise unusable as a hook.
items.reference: walkthrough_01_exterior-approach.mp4 is the Sora-2 golden-hour gimbal push-in to a contemporary two-storey facade; footage_summary tags it 'HOOK / establishing'.

items.question: Do the clips carry the expected technical shape: five landscape 1280x720 H.264 clips plus one vertical 720x1280 spa-bath clip, all ~8s with audio?
items.guidance: 5 = dimensions/orientation/duration as manifested, the bath clip genuinely vertical. 3 = minor deviation. 1 = wrong codec, wrong durations, or the vertical clip is actually landscape.
items.reference: manifest dims: clips 01-05 = 1280x720, clip 06 = 720x1280 vertical, all duration_s 8.0, has_audio true, mp4/H.264.

input_assets.what_it_is: A deliberately noisy agent narration recorded over a rough music/ambient bed with roomy reverb, supplied so the speech-cleanup leg has real audio to isolate and de-reverb into a broadcast dialogue stem.

items.question: Is the supplied VO genuinely degraded (audible music/ambient bed and reverb tail) so that the de-reverb / speech-isolation step has something real to fix?
items.guidance: Yes = clearly hears a bed and/or reverb under the speech, i.e. a real cleanup target. No = already clean/dry studio VO with nothing to isolate.
items.reference: agent_vo_rough.mp3 (~40.18s) is gpt-4o-mini-tts then roughened with added noise + convolution reverb per manifest; intended as a real isolate/de-reverb target.

items.question: Does the spoken narration match the brief's narration_script word-for-word (and the supplied transcript .txt)?
items.guidance: Yes = audio narration and agent_vo_rough.txt match campaign_brief.json narration_script verbatim. No = paraphrased, truncated, or different copy.
items.reference: agent_vo_rough.txt and the narration_script are identical: 'Discover an oasis where every detail elevates your daily life! ... uncover your new beginning.' No prices read aloud.

input_assets.display: Listing stills to recover/composite (hero interior + overcast exterior)
input_assets.what_it_is: The two photoreal stills the editor must rescue: a hero living room with genuinely blown-out windows and slightly off-plumb verticals, and a front exterior under a flat overcast sky with a clean top region to swap for twilight.

items.question: On the hero interior, are the tall windows genuinely blown out to near-pure white (outdoor view lost / highlights clipped) with the room sitting a touch dark and visibly mixed warm-lamp/cool-window white balance?
items.guidance: 5 = a true rescue target: windows clipped to white with no view, interior slightly underexposed, mixed WB obvious, verticals slightly crooked. 1 = windows already balanced with visible exterior, even WB — nothing to recover.
items.reference: hero-interior-living-room.jpg (1536x1024): a five-pane window wall blown to near-white with no outdoor view, warm tungsten lamp at left vs cool window light, throw blanket bunched on the grey sectional, open book on the round coffee table — exactly the raw frame to rescue (vision_score 10).

items.question: On the exterior, is the sky a flat featureless overcast grey-white with a clean uninterrupted region along the top third (roofline meeting blank sky) that is an obvious candidate to mask and replace with a twilight plate?
items.guidance: 5 = dull cloudless grey-white sky, clean roofline/sky boundary, easy to mask. 3 = sky has some texture/clouds complicating the mask. 1 = sky already coloured/dramatic, no clean mask region.
items.reference: exterior-front-overcast.jpg (1536x1024): pale stucco-and-cedar two-storey home under a flat washed-out grey-white sky, clean sky band across the top — ideal twilight-swap target (accepted_with_warnings).

items.question: Does the exterior still contain a legible house number on the entry pillar (a known 'no text' QC warning the editor may need to retouch out)?
items.guidance: Yes = a readable house number is visible on the pillar by the front door. No = no readable text anywhere on the facade.
items.reference: The number '209' is clearly visible on the pillar beside the front door of exterior-front-overcast.jpg — the manifest flags this as the residual QC issue.

input_assets.display: Twilight colour-reference frame + brokerage badge detail
input_assets.what_it_is: Two supporting stills: a deep blue-hour street frame that sets the dusk colour target for the composite, and a brushed-metal brokerage badge plaque carrying the doorway/keyhole emblem as a detail plate.

items.question: Does the twilight reference frame read as a usable dusk/blue-hour colour target (warm amber horizon graduating up through magenta to deep indigo, warm glowing windows, cool shadows)?
items.guidance: 5 = rich believable blue-hour gradient that clearly sets a grading target. 3 = dusk but flat or off-palette. 1 = not twilight at all (daylight/overcast).
items.reference: twilight-reference-frame.jpg (1536x1024): a residential street at deep twilight with an amber-to-magenta-to-indigo sky, backlit clouds, warm-lit windows on the right-hand house — a strong dusk colour reference (manifest notes it as mood/colour reference, low vision_score because it diverged from a mis-stated overcast prompt, but it is on-target for twilight).

items.question: Is the brokerage badge a brushed-metal plaque engraved with a simple abstract open-doorway/keyhole mark and NO readable lettering or brand words?
items.guidance: Yes = clean geometric doorway/keyhole emblem, no legible words, with realistic brushed grain/scratches/screws and a soft contact shadow. No = readable text, real-brand lookalike, or CGI-glossy.
items.reference: brokerage-badge-detail.jpg (1536x1024): a brushed-metal plaque with four corner screws, fine scratches/fingerprints and a cut-out open-doorway-with-keyhole emblem, no lettering — matches the abstract-mark spec (vision_score 9).

input_assets.what_it_is: The flat brokerage logo lockup on solid white — navy open-doorway framing a sage keyhole above the 'Solstice Properties / BOUTIQUE RESIDENTIAL' wordmark — supplied to background-remove and vectorize for the lower-third / logo bug.

items.question: Is the logo a clean flat lockup with the correct brand palette (deep ink navy mark/wordmark, single fresh-sage accent inside the doorway) on a solid white background suitable for background-removal and vectorizing?
items.guidance: 5 = crisp flat doorway/keyhole mark in navy with a sage keyhole, navy wordmark, solid white field, no gradients/shadows/photo elements. 3 = colours slightly off or minor extra detail. 1 = wrong colours, gradients, or busy background.
items.reference: brokerage_logo.png (1024x1024): navy (#1F2A3C-ish) open-door mark framing a sage (#7E9C7A-ish) keyhole on white, with 'Solstice Properties' wordmark and 'BOUTIQUE RESIDENTIAL' tagline; manifest notes the navy/sage are slightly off and a small door-handle detail is added (vision_score 9).

items.question: Is the wordmark spelled exactly 'Solstice Properties' with the tagline 'BOUTIQUE RESIDENTIAL'?
items.guidance: Yes = both strings spelled correctly with no typos or extra words. No = misspelling, wrong words, or garbled lettering.
items.reference: brokerage_logo.png reads 'Solstice Properties' over 'BOUTIQUE RESIDENTIAL', both correct.

outputs.display: Narrated tour MP4s (16:9 master + Feed 1:1 + Reels/Stories 9:16)
outputs.what_it_is: The story-ordered property tour cut from the six room clips with a 3-second exterior hook, cleaned VO over a ducked music bed and an animated lower-third, exported H.264 in 1280x720, 1080x1080 and 1080x1920.

items.question: Are all three required deliverable sizes present and technically correct — a 16:9 master plus Feed 1:1 (1080x1080) and a vertical 9:16 (1080x1920), all H.264 video with an AAC audio track?
items.guidance: 5 = all three exist, dimensions exact, every file plays with both video (avc1) and audio (mp4a). 3 = one size missing or one aspect wrong. 1 = wrong codecs, no audio, or only one export.
items.reference: tour_master_16x9.mp4 (1280x720), tour_feed_1x1.mp4 (1080x1080), tour_reels_stories_9x16.mp4 (1080x1920); all contain avc1 (H.264) + mp4a (AAC) atoms (verified). Note: brief lists Feed 1:1, Stories 9:16, Reels 9:16; the two 9:16 sizes are delivered as one shared 9:16 file.

items.question: Does the cut lead with a 3-second exterior golden-hour hook and then walk the rooms in a sensible tour order, with clean crossfades and no on-screen captions baked into the room footage?
items.guidance: 5 = exterior establishing hook in the first ~3s, then kitchen/living/hallway/primary-suite/spa-bath in a logical order, smooth transitions, no stray captions. 3 = hook present but order awkward or one hard cut. 1 = no hook or jumbled order.
items.reference: README + footage_summary chosen_tour_order: exterior-approach (HOOK) -> kitchen -> living-room -> hallway -> primary-suite -> primary-bath, hook_seconds 3.0, 0.4s crossfades.

items.question: Is the audio bed a cleaned, intelligible agent VO sitting clearly on top of a ducked music/ambient bed (not buried, not clipping), consistent across all three exports?
items.guidance: 5 = VO clean and forward, music ducked under it, levels consistent across sizes. 3 = VO present but bed too loud or levels inconsistent. 1 = VO buried/distorted or no music bed.
items.reference: README: cleaned VO muxed over a ducked warm ambient bed. KNOWN LIMITATION: the music bed is a local warm-ambient placeholder, not the final licensed Stock track (Adobe Stock audio returned no warm acoustic bed) — to be swapped for delivery. Score the mix as built; note the placeholder.

items.question: Does the tour carry a branded lower-third (logo bug + agent name/title + price strip) consistent with the brief's copy?
items.guidance: Yes = an on-brand lower-third with the keyhole bug, 'Eleanor Vance / Senior Sales Associate' and the just-listed price strip is visible over the footage. No = no lower-third or wrong/missing copy.
items.reference: README deliverable 1 + thumbnail show the animated lower-third: keyhole logo bug, 'Eleanor Vance · Senior Sales Associate', 'Just Listed - $975,000'.

outputs.what_it_is: The broadcast-ready dialogue stem isolated from the rough VO's music/ambient bed and de-reverbed, loudness-normalized for the mix.

items.question: Compared to the rough input, is the music/ambient bed substantially removed and the reverb tail suppressed, leaving a clear dialogue stem at broadcast loudness?
items.guidance: 5 = noticeably drier, isolated speech with the bed gone and a controlled loudness; few artefacts. 3 = improved but residual bed/reverb or pumping artefacts. 1 = no audible improvement over the rough input.
items.reference: README deliverable 2: band-limit + 2-pass FFT denoise (strips the bed) + anlmdn/compand (de-reverb) + loudnorm to -16 LUFS.

items.question: Is the stem delivered as a clean WAV (plus MP3) at a broadcast-sane format and matching the source duration?
items.guidance: Yes = a 48kHz/16-bit mono WAV ~40s plus an MP3, matching the ~40s rough VO. No = wrong duration, truncated, or only a lossy file.
items.reference: agent_vo_cleaned_stem.wav = 48000 Hz / 16-bit / mono / 40.11s (matches the 40.18s rough VO); agent_vo_cleaned_stem.mp3 also present.

outputs.what_it_is: The per-clip key-segment summary, chosen cut order, hook length and rough VO transcript that justify how the rooms were ordered.

items.question: Does the summary give a per-clip key segment and a defensible rationale for the chosen room order and the 3-second hook?
items.guidance: 5 = each of the 6 clips has a room + key_segment + tour_role, an explicit chosen_tour_order, hook_seconds, and prose explaining why the exterior leads. 3 = order present but thinly justified. 1 = no per-clip detail or no chosen order.
items.reference: footage_summary_and_transcript.json: 6 clips each with room/key_segment/tour_role, chosen_tour_order leading with exterior-approach, hook_seconds 3.0, and a footage_summary paragraph explaining the exterior leads as the only strong establishing beat.

items.question: Does the included rough transcript match the agent narration verbatim?
items.guidance: Yes = vo_rough_transcript equals the brief narration_script. No = paraphrased or different.
items.reference: vo_rough_transcript in the JSON matches the narration_script word-for-word.

outputs.what_it_is: The hero living room straightened, cropped, exposure/white-balance corrected, with the blown windows recovered via masked highlight/exposure edits and finished to the campaign 'listing look' preset.

items.question: Have the genuinely blown windows been recovered from near-pure white into legible, textured panels while the rest of the room stays naturally bright and airy?
items.guidance: 5 = windows pulled back to soft daylight-grey textured panes (no longer clipped white), room brighter/balanced, no obvious halo around the window frames. 3 = partial recovery or visible masking halo. 1 = windows still blown or recovery looks fake/dirty.
items.reference: hero_interior_graded.png (1506x1004): the five window panes that were pure-white in the source now read as soft textured daylight-grey blinds/panels; README flags this as the honest recovered tone (not a fabricated exterior view).

items.question: Is the image levelled (verticals plumb after straighten/crop) and white-balanced to a single warm bright-airy listing look, fixing the source's mixed warm-lamp/cool-window cast?
items.guidance: 5 = window mullions and wall edges read vertical, even warm-neutral WB, bright airy tone. 3 = slightly off-level or residual colour cast. 1 = still crooked or mixed WB.
items.reference: README deliverable 4: straighten -> crop -> auto-tone -> warm WB -> exposure -> masked recovery -> HSL -> vibrance -> Color-Bright preset (a real Adobe connector chain).

items.question: Does the graded hero serve as the single 'listing look' source whose grade is shared by every cover and the thumbnail?
items.guidance: Yes = the same warm-WB + Color-Bright grade visibly carries into the covers and thumbnail. No = covers/thumbnail show a different, inconsistent grade.
items.reference: README deliverable 9: the single graded hero (warm WB + Color-Bright) is the source for every cover and the thumbnail.

outputs.what_it_is: The exterior with its flat overcast sky masked out and a licensed Adobe Stock twilight plate composited behind it, the house dusk-graded, plus 1:1 / 9:16 / 16:9 cover crops.

items.question: Is the overcast sky cleanly replaced with a twilight plate and the house dusk-graded so the composite reads as one believable dusk photograph (consistent light direction, clean roofline mask, no fringing)?
items.guidance: 5 = convincing dusk: warm/indigo sky behind a correctly dusk-toned house, tight roofline mask, warm window glow, no halo or sky bleeding through trees. 3 = believable but with a visible mask edge or a tonal mismatch between sky and house. 1 = obvious paste-in, harsh fringe, or daylight house under a night sky.
items.reference: twilight_exterior_composite.png (1536x1024): the flat grey sky is replaced with a pink/orange-and-blue dusk sky and the house is warm-graded with lit windows; README credits licensed AdobeStock_227883293 as the sky plate. Inspect tree edges and the roofline for masking artefacts.

items.question: Are the twilight cover crops delivered at the campaign sizes (1:1 1080x1080, 9:16 1080x1920) sharing the same dusk composite without awkwardly cropping the house?
items.guidance: 5 = Feed 1:1 and Stories 9:16 covers both present at exact pixel sizes, house well-framed in each. 3 = one size off or house badly cropped. 1 = missing a required size.
items.reference: twilight_cover_feed_1x1.png = 1080x1080, twilight_cover_stories_9x16.png = 1080x1920, twilight_cover_16x9.png = 1200x675 (a 16:9 web crop, off the 1080-grid).

items.question: Was the source house number ('209') handled in the twilight composite (either retouched out or at least not newly distracting)?
items.guidance: Yes = no distracting legible house number remains, or it has been cleanly removed. No = the '209' is still plainly legible on the pillar.
items.reference: The input exterior carries a legible '209' on the entry pillar; the twilight_exterior_composite still shows it. Flag if a 'no-text' delivery requires it removed.

outputs.what_it_is: The brokerage logo background-removed and vectorized to a clean SVG/cutout, plus transparent lower-third overlays (logo bug + agent strip + price pill) sized for 9:16 and 1:1.

items.question: Is the logo mark cleanly background-removed and vectorized to an SVG with smooth paths in the correct navy + sage palette (no white box, no jaggies)?
items.guidance: 5 = crisp vector doorway/keyhole on transparency, navy door + sage keyhole, smooth curves. 3 = usable but rough edges or slightly off colour. 1 = raster halo, white box, or broken shape.
items.reference: brokerage_logo_vector.svg contains real vector paths filled rgb(27,43,65) navy (~#1B2B41) and rgb(125,154,118) sage (~#7D9A76); brokerage_logo_mark.png (244x285) is the navy-door + sage-keyhole cutout on transparency. KNOWN LIMITATION: vectorization kept only the icon mark and dropped the 'Solstice Properties' wordmark text — the name is re-set in brand type in the lockups.

items.question: Do the lower-third overlays typeset the exact brief copy — agent 'Eleanor Vance', title 'SENIOR SALES ASSOCIATE' and price '$975,000' — with the logo bug and brand-coloured pill, on a transparent background?
items.guidance: 5 = all strings spelled exactly right, navy pill + oak/sage accents + keyhole bug, transparent PNG ready to overlay. 3 = correct copy but off-palette or opaque background. 1 = wrong copy, typos, or wrong price.
items.reference: lower_third_9x16.png (1080x1920) and lower_third_1x1.png (1080x1080): transparent PNGs with a navy pill, oak/sage accent bar, keyhole bug, 'Eleanor Vance' / 'SENIOR SALES ASSOCIATE' and a '$975,000' oak price pill.

items.question: Is the lower-third type set to the font_recommend output (brand grotesque or its closest installed substitute) rather than a default system serif?
items.guidance: Yes = clean geometric grotesque consistent with Libre Franklin/Inter (or the documented Avenir Next/Helvetica Neue substitute). No = mismatched/default serif or inconsistent fonts.
items.reference: README: font_recommend drove the type; brand fonts (Libre Franklin/Inter) not installed, so set in closest grotesques (Avenir Next/Helvetica Neue) — substitute brand fonts for final delivery.

outputs.what_it_is: The graded hero generative-expanded and reframed to fill 1:1 and 9:16 covers without cropping the room, branded with the lower-third/headline, plus a 1280x720 video thumbnail with a play button.

items.question: Are the covers reframed to 1:1 and 9:16 by generative-expanding the ceiling/floor (not by cropping into the room), at exact pixel sizes, sharing the hero grade?
items.guidance: 5 = Feed 1:1 (1080x1080) and Stories/Reels 9:16 (1080x1920) covers with the full room intact and seamless expanded ceiling/floor, same warm grade. 3 = sizes right but a visible expand seam or tight crop. 1 = room cropped off or wrong sizes.
items.reference: cover_feed_1x1.png = 1080x1080, cover_stories_9x16.png and cover_reels_9x16.png = 1080x1920 (identical bytes, the shared 9:16 cover); README deliverable 7: generative-expand (ceiling+floor) then 9:16 crop. The 9:16 covers show expanded ceiling/floor above and below the recovered windows.

items.question: Do the covers carry the correct campaign copy and branding — 'SOLSTICE PROPERTIES' + 'Your Home, Elevated.', '1234 Serene Haven Way', the 'WILLOW CREEK ESTATES · 4 BD · 3 BA · 2,850 SQFT' subline, agent strip and '$975,000' pill — legibly placed over the hero?
items.guidance: 5 = all strings present, correctly spelled, brand-coloured, legible against the image. 3 = present but one string wrong/clipped or low contrast. 1 = missing branding or wrong data (e.g. wrong price/sqft).
items.reference: cover_feed_1x1 and cover_stories_9x16 show the SOLSTICE PROPERTIES bug + 'YOUR HOME, ELEVATED.', headline '1234 Serene Haven Way', subline 'WILLOW CREEK ESTATES · 4 BD · 3 BA · 2,850 SQFT', 'Eleanor Vance / SENIOR SALES ASSOCIATE' strip and '$975,000' pill — all matching campaign_brief.json. (On the 1:1 the navy headline crosses the bright window and is slightly hard to read — note legibility.)

items.question: Is the video thumbnail delivered at 1280x720 with the same grade/branding and a clear play affordance and 'JUST LISTED' / price marker?
items.guidance: 5 = 1280x720, shares the hero grade, has a centered play button, logo, headline, 'JUST LISTED' pill and 'Just Listed - $975,000'. 3 = right size but missing play button or a copy element. 1 = wrong size or no branding.
items.reference: video_thumbnail_1280x720.png = 1280x720 with logo top-left, 'JUST LISTED' pill top-right, headline '1234 Serene Haven Way', subline, centered play button, and 'Eleanor Vance / SENIOR SALES ASSOCIATE · SOLSTICE PROPERTIES' + 'JUST LISTED - $975,000' lower strip.

task_level.question: Across the whole package, are all nine asked deliverables present — narrated tour MP4 (Feed/Stories/Reels), cleaned VO stem, footage summary, graded hero, twilight composite cover, vectorized logo + lower-third, generative-expanded 1:1/9:16 covers + thumbnail, and a consistent grade?
task_level.guidance: 5 = every deliverable group present and on-brief. 3 = one deliverable missing or only partially met. 1 = multiple deliverables missing.
task_level.reference: outputs/ contains 3 tour MP4s, cleaned VO wav+mp3, footage_summary json+txt, hero_interior_graded.png, twilight_exterior_composite + 3 twilight covers, brokerage_logo_vector.svg + mark + 2 lower-thirds, 3 graded covers + 1280x720 thumbnail — all 9 README deliverables produced.

task_level.question: Do the video thumbnail and every still share one consistent aesthetic (white balance, tone, the Color-Bright preset) so the campaign reads as a single visual system?
task_level.guidance: 5 = thumbnail and all covers visibly share the warm bright-airy hero grade. 3 = mostly consistent with one off-grade asset. 1 = grades clearly differ across assets.
task_level.reference: README deliverable 9: the single graded hero (warm WB + Color-Bright) is the source for every cover and the thumbnail; verify the feed/stories covers and thumbnail match the hero tone.

task_level.question: Is every piece of typeset copy across covers, thumbnail and lower-thirds faithful to campaign_brief.json (address '1234 Serene Haven Way', '$975,000', 4 BD / 3 BA / 2,850 SQFT, 'Eleanor Vance', 'Senior Sales Associate', 'Solstice Properties', 'Your Home, Elevated.') with no typos or mismatched numbers?
task_level.guidance: 5 = all strings/numbers match the brief everywhere. 3 = one minor inconsistency (e.g. casing). 1 = a wrong price, sqft, or agent detail anywhere.
task_level.reference: campaign_brief.json: address '1234 Serene Haven Way', price '$975,000', beds 4 / baths 3 / sqft 2850, agent 'Eleanor Vance, Senior Sales Associate'. Covers, thumbnail and lower-thirds all reproduce these.

task_level.question: Was the still + branding leg actually produced through the Adobe connector image chain (straighten/crop/auto-tone/WB/exposure/masked recovery/HSL/vibrance/preset/generative-expand/resize/select/fill/remove-background/vectorize + stock license + font_recommend), per the trajectory?
task_level.guidance: Yes = the trajectory shows the real connector ops driving the hero grade, twilight sky license, logo vectorize and font_recommend. No = the image leg was faked locally with no connector calls.
task_level.reference: trajectory.json: 42 steps, 25 adobe_connector ops covering the full image grade chain, asset_search + asset_license_and_download_stock (AdobeStock_227883293 twilight sky), remove_background, vectorize and font_recommend; 9 local_video + 7 local_compositor + 1 verify.

task_level.question: Are the package's honest limitations clearly disclosed and bounded (video/audio done locally via ffmpeg because connector video tools aren't retrievable headless; music bed is a placeholder; brand fonts substituted; wordmark dropped from the vector; windows recovered to a grey panel not a fabricated view)?
task_level.guidance: 5 = every limitation is documented with a clear 'swap for delivery' path and none silently overclaim Adobe where it was local. 3 = limitations noted but vague. 1 = limitations hidden or the README overclaims.
task_level.reference: outputs/README.md 'Honest limitations' lists: video is local ffmpeg (connector video tools unretrievable headless), music bed is a warm-ambient placeholder (sky plate IS real licensed Stock), brand fonts substituted, logo vector kept the mark and dropped the wordmark, stills cap at 1536px, windows recovered to honest grey panels.

task_level.question: Taken as a whole, would this package work as a real lead-gen listing drop — a hooky narrated tour plus matched branded covers/thumbnail across Feed/Stories/Reels — that a boutique brokerage could post with only the documented final swaps (licensed music, brand fonts)?
task_level.guidance: 5 = cohesive, on-brand, platform-correct set ready to post after the two documented swaps. 3 = usable but needs noticeable cleanup (e.g. cover legibility, residual house number). 1 = not deliverable without redoing core work.
task_level.reference: Package delivers a 3-size narrated tour with a 3s hook + cleaned VO, a recovered/graded hero, a believable twilight cover, branded lower-thirds/thumbnail and exact-size covers sharing one grade; residual nits to weigh: placeholder music, substitute fonts, dropped wordmark text, the '$975,000' headline contrast on the 1:1 feed cover, and the still-legible '209' on the twilight exterior.
